macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin extends its pullback as crypto markets stay under pressure
items.summary: Bitcoin traded around $73,252, down 0.3% over 24 hours, after losing its breakout attempt near $82,000 and falling below $74,000 to a six-week low. CryptoPotato also reported Bitcoin near $73,140, with broader market volatility and several altcoins showing mixed performance.

items.title: FalconX files confidential IPO paperwork with the SEC
items.summary: Crypto trading firm FalconX submitted a confidential initial public offering filing to the U.S. SEC, signaling a possible public listing later this year depending on market conditions. The move is one of the most notable institutional-development stories in crypto over the past day.

items.title: SoFi launches bank-issued stablecoin inside its consumer app
items.summary: SoFi rolled out SoFiUSD to its 14.7 million members, becoming the first U.S. national bank to embed a bank-issued stablecoin directly into a consumer banking app. The launch is a major product milestone for regulated digital assets and bank-integrated crypto services.

items.title: Paxos secures another regulatory win tied to stablecoins
items.summary: Paxos received what the report describes as a regulatory win as it continues expanding its stablecoin and tokenization business. The same coverage notes its earlier technical error with PYUSD and its subsequent conditional approval to convert into an OCC national trust bank.

items.title: Tether’s U.S.-focused stablecoin grows sharply month over month
items.summary: Tether’s USAT stablecoin reportedly surged more than 500% month-over-month in April, with supply rising to $140.8 million by April 30 from $22 million at the end of March. The rapid growth highlights continued stablecoin expansion in the U.S.-linked market.

items.title: Hyperliquid’s HYPE hits a new all-time high
items.summary: CryptoPotato reported HYPE reaching a fresh all-time high above $73, making it one of the standout large-cap movers in the latest session. The token’s strength contrasted with weaker Bitcoin and Ethereum trading.

items.title: XRP Ledger activity rises despite XRP price weakness
items.summary: CryptoPotato highlighted Messari data showing XRP Ledger transactions and stablecoin adoption increased in Q1 2026 even as XRP fell 27% during the quarter. The development suggests network activity is improving despite token underperformance.
